title: Perityle crassifolia var. robusta
what_description: Perityle crassifolia var. robusta is a plant taxon in the Asteraceae family. It is a shrub-like perennial herb with a woody base, typically 30-60 cm in height. The leaves are elliptical to lanceolate, 1-4 cm long, 0.2-1.5 cm wide, with a crenulate margin, glabrous to pubescent, and often with a reddish tinge. The inflorescence is a solitary capitate head, 1-2 cm in diameter, with 5-15 yellow flowers, each 0.5-1 cm in diameter, with 5-7 corolla lobes. The plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Perityle crassifolia var. robusta are inflammation, skin irritation, and wound healing, which are alleviated by its anti-inflammatory properties. Fungal infections and bacterial infections, such as infections caused by Staphylococcus aureus, are treated due to its antifungal and antibacterial properties. Perityle crassifolia var. robusta also exhibits antioxidant properties, which aid in pain relief and digestive issues by neutralizing free radicals. Additionally, its antiviral properties help alleviate fever, diarrhea, and cough. Furthermore, its antifungal and antibacterial properties also help in treating skin conditions, allergies, and rheumatism, including arthritis.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Perityle crassifolia var. robusta are its antioxidant properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. This plant exhibits significant antimicrobial activity, with saponins and volatile oils contributing to its ability to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms. The anti-inflammatory properties of Perityle crassifolia var. robusta are due to the bioactive compounds such as terpenoids and triterpenoids, which reduce inflammation and alleviate associated symptoms. Additionally, the analgesic properties of this plant are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, which provide pain relief. Furthermore, the antifungal properties of Perityle crassifolia var. robusta are associated with the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds, which inhibit the growth of various fungi.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Perityle crassifolia var. robusta are flavonoids such as kaempferol and quercetin that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, phenolic acids including rosmarinic acid with antimicrobial and ant-inflammatory effects, triterpenoids like ursolic acid that exhibit anti-inflammatory and anticancer activities, saponins including oleanolic acid with hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, and tannins which have antimicrobial and astringent effects contributing to its healing potential. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Perityle crassifolia var. robusta are known to scavenge free radicals and exhibit antioxidant activity. The triterpenoids, particularly ursolic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and reduce inflammation. The saponins in this plant have been found to protect the liver from damage and reduce inflammation. The tannins present in Perityle crassifolia var. robusta contribute to its antimicrobial and astringent properties.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Perityle crassifolia var. robusta are tea, infusion, tincture, salve, and decoction. Perityle crassifolia var. robusta tea is made by steeping dried leaves in hot water, used for colds and sore throats due to its antimicrobial properties. Infusion involves steeping the leaves in cold water to make a tea, used for fever reduction and as a digestive aid. Tincture is prepared by soaking the leaves in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin, used for its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. Decoction involves boiling the leaves in water to extract its medicinal compounds, used for respiratory issues and as an expectorant.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Perityle crassifolia var. robusta improperly include experiencing nausea and cause nausea due to its toxic glycosides, which can induce seizures in individuals with pre-existing seizure disorders or those who take medications that lower the seizure threshold, thereby interacting with medications such as anticoagulants, anti-seizure drugs, and certain antidepressants, and may also cause gastrointestinal symptoms like experiencing diarrhea.
